name: Gravity_Mech

infobox.Systems: Grav Well(Science)EffectArtillery weapon that pulls its target towards you.Vek Hormones(Passive)EffectEnemies do +1 Damage against other enemies.Upgrade I+1 Damage ()Increases damage by 1.Upgrade II+1 Damage ()Increases damage by 1.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theSteel Judokasquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Gravity Mech is useful for turning the Vek against each other, thanks to its Vek Hormones Passive that it comes equipped with, which effectively makes the enemy deal high amounts of damage for the player. It can be placed in many suitable combinations forCustom Squadsthanks to this passive.
The Grav Well weapon can be used to pull Vek towards it, disrupting attacks and positioning them to attack other enemies. It's a basic weapon much likeAttraction Pulse, although note that this attack is lobbed like an artillery projectile; while it is not able to pull enemies into the Gravity Mech for bump damage, it is possible to pull distant enemies into each other or onto structures such as Mountains.
The Vek Hormones makes all enemies — includingSentient Weaponsandminor enemies— deal extra damage to each other. It can also be further upgraded in damage, scaling very well in higher difficulties where more Alpha enemies are present. The first+1 Damageupgrade is worth getting as soon as possible, as it lets all Alpha enemies deal 4-5 damage to enemies, which is enough to let them destroy other Alphas. The second damage upgrade is more costly at 2 Reactor Cores and has less valuable return, but it is still useful for letting other Alphas take down Vek Leaders (most of which have 6HP); this is most useful inUnfair difficultyduring the 4th island andVolcanic Hive, which have an extra Vek Leader on each mission.
Vek Hormones overall works well with the Steel Judoka squad, as all its Mechs have weapons that can manipulate enemy positions; in this squad, it's advisable to move the passive to theSiege Mechso that the Gravity Mech can have another open slot for a more useful weapon. If using the Grav Mech in aCustom Squad, it is recommended to similarly pair it with Mechs that excel at manipulating enemy positions and moving the passive off to one of them; notable examples including theControl MechandExchange Mech.
Because of how well the extra damage Vek deal to each other scales, look for islands with enemies that fire in a straight line such asFirefilesandGastropods.
sections.Trivia: - The Vek Hormones passive equipped with the Gravity Mech also increases the damage done byrobotic enemies, despite not being classified as Vek nor affected by Psions.

The Gravity Mech is useful for turning the Vek against each other, thanks to its Vek Hormones Passive that it comes equipped with, which effectively makes the enemy deal high amounts of damage for the player. It can be placed in many suitable combinations for
Custom Squads
thanks to this passive.
The Grav Well weapon can be used to pull Vek towards it, disrupting attacks and positioning them to attack other enemies. It's a basic weapon much like
Attraction Pulse
, although note that this attack is lobbed like an artillery projectile; while it is not able to pull enemies into the Gravity Mech for bump damage, it is possible to pull distant enemies into each other or onto structures such as Mountains.
The Vek Hormones makes all enemies — including
Sentient Weapons
and
minor enemies
— deal extra damage to each other. It can also be further upgraded in damage, scaling very well in higher difficulties where more Alpha enemies are present. The first
+1 Damage
upgrade is worth getting as soon as possible, as it lets all Alpha enemies deal 4-5 damage to enemies, which is enough to let them destroy other Alphas. The second damage upgrade is more costly at 2 Reactor Cores and has less valuable return, but it is still useful for letting other Alphas take down Vek Leaders (most of which have 6HP); this is most useful in
Unfair difficulty
during the 4th island and
Volcanic Hive
, which have an extra Vek Leader on each mission.
Vek Hormones overall works well with the Steel Judoka squad, as all its Mechs have weapons that can manipulate enemy positions; in this squad, it's advisable to move the passive to the
Siege Mech
so that the Gravity Mech can have another open slot for a more useful weapon. If using the Grav Mech in a
Custom Squad
, it is recommended to similarly pair it with Mechs that excel at manipulating enemy positions and moving the passive off to one of them; notable examples including the
Control Mech
and
Exchange Mech
.
Because of how well the extra damage Vek deal to each other scales, look for islands with enemies that fire in a straight line such as
Firefiles
and
Gastropods

Trivia

The Vek Hormones passive equipped with the Gravity Mech also increases the damage done by
robotic enemies
, despite not being classified as Vek nor affected by Psions.
